cross_scene_arcs.title: From Unemployed Dreamer to Reluctant Co-Creator
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney transitions from joblessness and self-doubt to becoming a co-owner of Fibrerock, though his contributions are initially unrecognized and his role shaped more by circumstance than credit.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: unemployed, asserting identity through invented achievements
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: gains 0.5% profit share in Fibrerock, moving into financial stability
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: lack of formal recognition for his inventive role

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ethical Voice Amid Group Pressure
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney repeatedly acts as a moral and technical conscience, opposing sabotage, raising safety concerns, and identifying critical flaws in Fibrerock, often at personal cost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: sole dissenter from Buffaloes' sabotage plan
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: identifies coo-coo berry sap deficiency as cause of structural failure
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: his warnings are initially ignored or dismissed by authority figures

cross_scene_arcs.title: Crisis Leadership and Technical Problem-Solving
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney shifts into a leadership role during the city's collapse, using his technical insight and tools to guide evacuation and realize the commercial potential of transformed Fibrerock.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: identifies missing coo-coo berry sap as structural risk
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: co-leader in evacuation and co-beneficiary of Fibrerock's transformation into a marketable product
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: long-term safety and legal implications of Fibrerock's new properties remain unknown

cross_scene_arcs.title: Tension Between Invention and Recognition
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney's inventive contributions—constructing the car hood, co-developing Fibrerock, and diagnosing its flaw—are consistently downplayed or misattributed, though he eventually gains financial stake.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: inventor producing tangible results without credit
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: gains worldwide rights to Fibrerock alongside Fred
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: his role remains under-acknowledged despite critical technical contributions

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Provider to Innovator: Fred's Shifting Role at Work
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred transitions from a blue-collar worker to a celebrated innovator, then faces backlash and personal collapse before ultimately achieving economic independence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: ends work shift at quarry, transitions to family leisure mode
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: elevated from employee to owner, gains worldwide rights to Fibrerock
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: potential legal liabilities from the destruction caused during the transformation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fred and Barney's Fractured Collaboration
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred and Barney's friendship evolves through shared domestic chaos, collaborative invention, betrayal over credit, and eventual reconciliation under crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: shares darkly comic moment with Wilma about misfortune
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: reconciliation with Barney after public humiliation and arrogance
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether Fred will sustain humility after the crisis passes

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Success: Public Recognition vs. Worker Loyalty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred is credited for Fibrerock's success but is blamed by coworkers when it leads to layoffs, creating a conflict between his personal advancement and group loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred is isolated and emotionally defeated
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Fred must recover from personal defeat and regain credibility with the workers

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fred's Morning Routine and Domestic Instability
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred's attempts to maintain domestic and personal control are repeatedly disrupted by Dino, Barney, and mechanical failures, undermining his sense of stability.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Barney's unemployment and erratic behavior continue to disrupt Fred's attempts to maintain order

cross_scene_arcs.title: Narrative Control: Effort, Luck, and Self-Justification
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred consistently frames outcomes as the result of personal effort, resists acknowledging chance or others' contributions, but eventually acts decisively when truth and urgency align.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: believes in his own narrative of success
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred commits to action and leaves the workshop with Barney to save the people in Slate City
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: disagreement about the role of luck versus effort in their outcome

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Crisis and Loss of Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Slate transitions from authoritative control to visible destabilization as financial threats, operational failures, and personal outbursts erode his leadership stability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: assertive authority figure maintaining professional hierarchy
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: reactive and overwhelmed, grasping at innovation to offset mounting crises
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: company faces imminent bankruptcy and operational collapse

cross_scene_arcs.title: Exploitation and Erosion of Worker Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Slate enacts cost-cutting layoffs using Fibrerock as justification, shifts blame to Fred, and uses psychological pressure to remove him from PR duties, deepening worker alienation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: implementing labor replacement to increase profits
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred isolated by coworkers, Slate maintains control through coercion
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: worker resentment and Fred's role as scapegoat remain unaddressed

cross_scene_arcs.title: Legacy, Naming, and Blind Sign-Off
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Slate secures symbolic legacy through naming major structures after himself, only to lose them in collapse, reducing his monument to a salvaged statue head.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: proudly asserting identity through 'Slate Scrapers' and 'Slate Needle'
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: holding only the severed head of his statue after the collapse
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: emotional impact of symbolic loss deferred

cross_scene_arcs.title: Betrayal, Collapse, and Forced Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Slate discovers Lava's sabotage, confronts him physically, survives the building collapse, and ultimately acknowledges his mistreatment of Fred and Barney, relinquishing Fibrerock rights.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: furious at Lava's betrayal and intent on retaliation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: grateful and repentant, transferring full rights of Fibrerock to Fred and Barney
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: underestimates future liabilities despite visible destruction

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shifting Relations with Lava and Fred
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Slate's relationship with Lava deteriorates from professional reliance to violent hostility, while his view of Fred and Barney shifts from indifference to admiration to blame, ending in gratitude.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: reinforcing hierarchy with Lava, dismissing Fred and Barney
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: hostile toward Lava, deeply grateful to Fred and Barney
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: long-term trust dynamics remain uncertain after abrupt reversal
